content:
{
  "title": "Anti-Bias Prompt Ensembles: Dynamic Prompt Pooling for Social Bias Mitigation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often reinforce social biases and stereotypes, even when prompted to be neutral or fair. Existing prompting and self-debiasing techniques offer limited robustness across diverse demographic axes and prompt contexts.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs see increased deployment in sensitive applications, mitigating persistent, context-dependent social biases is critical for fairness and user trust. Prompt engineering offers a lightweight alternative to retraining, but current methods lack adaptability to shifting or intersectional bias factors. A dynamic, ensemble-based approach may enable more generalizable and robust debiasing.",
  "method": "We propose Anti-Bias Prompt Ensembles, a novel prompting method that dynamically pools and interleaves multiple bias-countering prompts for each input context. For each query, a set of anti-bias prompts—crafted to counteract specific stereotypes along gender, race, and intersectional dimensions—are selected and used to generate parallel LLM outputs. These outputs are then aggregated using an uncertainty-aware voting mechanism that prioritizes responses with lower bias signals (as detected by an automatic bias classifier). Our method adaptively updates the prompt pool based on feedback from detected biases in generated responses, continually refining the anti-bias ensemble.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate on datasets measuring social bias (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, BOLD) and use standard bias metrics alongside fluency/knowledge benchmarks. Baselines include direct prompting, self-debiasing methods, and chain-of-thought debiasing. We measure reduction in stereotype scores, response diversity, and trade-offs in utility."
}
